approvalStep.description: Reference example for issue #125's Apex-trigger bridge alternative. Approval is assigned to the record owner's manager -- adjust to whatever assignedApprover fits your org (a queue, a named user, a role) before activating. This is a demo entry point: Revenant does not care how the approver is chosen, only that the process's final actions land on Approval_Status__c.
approvalStep.label: Manager Approval
approvalStep.name: Manager_Approval

ApprovalProcess.description: Reference example for issue #125: demonstrates bridging a native Approval Process to the durable NativeApprovalWorkflowExample workflow via a plain Apex trigger (CaseApprovalBridgeTrigger.trigger / NativeApprovalTriggerBridgeExample.cls) instead of Flow metadata -- an alternative to Revenant_Native_Approval_Example, which does the same job with two record-triggered Flows. Ships inactive as all Approval Processes do; activate after adjusting the approver assignment for your org. Final actions only flip Approval_Status__c -- CaseApprovalBridgeTrigger reacts to that change and signals the workflow directly in Apex. This is example-only wiring on the standard Case object: it fires on every Case in the org, which is acceptable for a reference example but would need scoping (e.g. a dedicated Record Type) before adapting for real use.
ApprovalProcess.label: Revenant Trigger Approval Example
